text.title: Canonical Folder Layout and Layer Responsibilities
text.summary: Defines the src/ directory structure, per-layer responsibilities, and the rule that process.env access is confined to config/index.ts.

rule.statement: Controllers: HTTP handling only — parse request params, validate input with Zod, call one service method, return the result, delegate all errors to next(). No business logic, no database access.
rule.rationale: Controllers that contain business logic cannot be unit-tested without an HTTP layer. Keeping controllers thin makes them trivial adapters that rarely need modification.

rule.statement: Services: business logic and orchestration — validation rules, multi-step operations, domain error throwing. Services receive domain types and return domain types. No Prisma imports, no HTTP concepts (req, res).
rule.rationale: Services are the only layer that knows the business rules. Isolating them from both HTTP and database concerns enables pure unit testing with mocked repositories and no infrastructure dependencies.

rule.statement: Repositories: all database access — Prisma queries, data mapping, connection management. Repositories implement typed interfaces (IUserRepository). No business logic, no HTTP concepts.
rule.rationale: Repository interfaces decouple services from ORM specifics. Swapping Prisma for another ORM requires changing only repository files, not services.

rule.statement: process.env access is allowed only in config/index.ts. Everywhere else, import from config. Never scatter process.env.XYZ across the codebase.
rule.rationale: Scattered environment variable access is invisible to type checking, cannot be validated at startup, and makes configuration changes require searching the entire codebase.

section.title: Project Structure
section.p: Canonical layout for src/: app.ts (Express setup), server.ts (entry), config/ (index.ts + database.ts), controllers/, services/, repositories/, models/, middleware/ (auth.ts, errorHandler.ts, validate.ts), routes/ (index.ts + per-entity), types/, utils/ (errors.ts, logger.ts).

rule.statement: Router files import only from container.ts and middleware. Never import services directly into route files.
rule.rationale: Router-to-service imports bypass the container, creating a second instantiation point. All instantiation must flow through container.ts as the single source of truth.
